recap:
- Context windows have hard limits (e.g., 8k, 32k, 128k tokens) that define the working memory available to models.
- The context window includes both input tokens (instructions, documents, history) and output tokens (model-generated text).
- Filling the context window with excessive data can degrade reasoning ability (the "Lost in the Middle" phenomenon) and increase latency.
- Token consumption has associated costs, making efficient management of token budgets essential for sustainability.
- Optimization strategies include Just-in-Time Retrieval, where only relevant sections are retrieved on demand, and Reranking, which selects the most relevant chunks for the context window.
- Balancing comprehensive context with cost efficiency and performance is crucial for effective application scaling.
